Title: Isolation and Functional Characterization of Two CONSTANS-like 16 (MiCOL16) Genes from Mango

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: MiCOL16A contains one B-box and one CCT domain and clusters phylogenetically with Arabidopsis AtCOL6 and AtCOL16 in group II of the CO gene family; its MR domain between B-box and CCT is required for transcriptional activation activity in yeast.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. MiCOL16A encodes a group II CONSTANS-like BBX protein with one B-box and one CCT domain and is closely related to AtCOL6/AtCOL16.
2. In mango, MiCOL16A is highly expressed in leaves, especially on nonflowering branches and during the late floral induction period, and shows a robust circadian rhythm with peaks after dusk under different photoperiods.
3. MiCOL16A protein localizes to the nucleus and exhibits transcriptional activation activity in yeast dependent on its MR domain.
4. Overexpression of MiCOL16A in Arabidopsis delays flowering under both LD and SD conditions, associated with reduced expression of AtFT and AtSOC1.
5. MiCOL16A overexpression enhances salt and drought tolerance in Arabidopsis, with longer primary roots and higher survival rates under NaCl and drought treatments, accompanied by increased expression of stress-responsive genes AtNHX1, AtRD20, AtSOS1 and AtCOR15A.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression delays flowering and enhances tolerance to salt and drought stress in Arabidopsis, indicating a negative role in flowering promotion and a positive role in abiotic stress responses for mango.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Acts as a nuclear transcriptional activator via its MR domain in yeast; overexpression represses expression of flowering integrator genes AtFT and AtSOC1 and increases expression of stress-responsive genes AtNHX1, AtRD20 and AtSOS1 under salt stress and AtCOR15A, AtRD20 and AtNHX1 under drought stress in Arabidopsis.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Photoperiodic flowering regulatory pathway and abiotic stress (salt and drought) response pathway.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: MiCOL16A --(represses expression)--> AtFT; MiCOL16A --(represses expression)--> AtSOC1; MiCOL16A --(upregulates expression under salt stress)--> AtNHX1; MiCOL16A --(upregulates expression under salt stress)--> AtRD20; MiCOL16A --(upregulates expression under salt stress)--> AtSOS1; MiCOL16A --(upregulates expression under drought stress)--> AtCOR15A; MiCOL16A --(upregulates expression under drought stress)--> AtRD20; MiCOL16A --(upregulates expression under drought stress)--> AtNHX1.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: CONSTANS-like (COL16) BBX family transcription factor with one B-box zinc finger domain and one CCT domain.
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus, as shown by 35S::MiCOL16A-GFP fusion transiently expressed in onion inner epidermal cells.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: In mango, MiCOL16A is expressed in all tested tissues, with highest expression in leaves and lowest in buds and flowers; expression in leaves of nonflowering branches is higher than in flowering branches and peaks during the late floral induction period before decreasing. MiCOL16A shows circadian oscillation with expression increasing after dusk, peaking about 6 h after dusk and then declining under LD, MD and SD photoperiods.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Photoperiod conditions (LD 16 h light/8 h dark, MD 12 h light/12 h dark, SD 8 h light/16 h dark); salt stress using NaCl (100–200 mM in agar media, 300 mM in soil drench); osmotic/drought stress using mannitol (300–500 mM) and water withholding.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Flowering time regulation and abiotic stress tolerance in mango.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Flowering time; abiotic stress tolerance (salt stress, drought stress).
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Under LD, MiCOL16A overexpression lines flowered at about 28.0–28.1 days versus 24.8 days in WT and had 6.3–7.5 versus 5.5 rosette leaves; under SD, they flowered at 51.3–52.8 days versus 50.1 days in WT (Table 1). Under 300 mM NaCl, 80.0–93.3% of MiCOL16A overexpression plants survived versus 20.0% WT; under drought, 73.3–93.3% overexpression plants survived versus 6.7% WT.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Altered plant stature at flowering in Arabidopsis overexpression lines and increased primary root length under NaCl and mannitol treatments compared with WT.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: M. indica L. cv. SiJiMi leaves, stems, buds and flowers; wild-type Arabidopsis thaliana ecotype Columbia (Col-0); Arabidopsis 35S::MiCOL16A overexpression lines; onion inner epidermal cells; Y2H Gold yeast strain; Agrobacterium tumefaciens EH105.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Cloning of MiCOL16A from SiJiMi mango transcriptomic data and genomic DNA; sequence and domain analysis; qRT-PCR for tissue-specific, temporal and circadian expression; subcellular localization via 35S::MiCOL16A-GFP transient expression; yeast one-hybrid transcriptional activation assay with full-length and MR-deletion constructs; generation of Arabidopsis 35S::MiCOL16A lines by floral dip; flowering time, rosette leaf number and plant height measurements under LD and SD; root growth assays on MS with NaCl or mannitol; whole-plant salt (300 mM NaCl) and drought (water withholding) treatments with survival scoring; qRT-PCR of AtFT, AtSOC1, AtNHX1, AtRD20, AtSOS1 and AtCOR15A.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: 35S::MiCOL16A overexpression in Arabidopsis thaliana Col-0 under LD and SD conditions with flowering time and plant height measurements; salt and drought stress assays (NaCl, mannitol, water withholding) assessing root length and survival; qRT-PCR analysis of AtFT, AtSOC1 and stress-responsive genes; subcellular localization of MiCOL16A-GFP in onion epidermal cells; yeast transcriptional activation assay of BD-MiCOL16A and MR-deletion construct.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified as a CONSTANS-like homolog from SiJiMi mango transcriptomic data, then cloned from cDNA and genomic DNA of M. indica cv. SiJiMi using gene-specific primers and sequencing validation.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Candidate gene for manipulating flowering time and improving salt and drought tolerance in mango breeding and cultivation, as its overexpression delays flowering and enhances abiotic stress tolerance in transgenic plants.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: MiCOL16B contains one B-box and one CCT domain and clusters with Arabidopsis AtCOL6 and AtCOL16 in group II of the CO gene family; its MR domain is required for transcriptional activation activity in yeast.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. MiCOL16B encodes a group II CONSTANS-like BBX protein with one B-box and one CCT domain and is closely related to AtCOL6/AtCOL16.
2. In mango, MiCOL16B is highly expressed in leaves, particularly on flowering branches and during the early floral induction period, with low expression in buds and flowers and no clear circadian rhythm.
3. MiCOL16B protein localizes to the nucleus and exhibits transcriptional activation activity in yeast dependent on its MR domain.
4. Overexpression of MiCOL16B in Arabidopsis delays flowering under both LD and SD conditions, associated with reduced expression of AtFT and AtSOC1.
5. MiCOL16B overexpression enhances salt and drought tolerance in Arabidopsis, with longer primary roots and higher survival rates under NaCl and drought treatments, accompanied by increased expression of stress-responsive genes AtNHX1, AtSOS1, AtRD20 and AtCOR15A.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression delays flowering and improves tolerance to salt and drought stress in Arabidopsis, indicating MiCOL16B negatively regulates flowering and positively contributes to abiotic stress responses in mango.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Acts as a nuclear transcriptional activator via its MR domain in yeast; overexpression represses expression of flowering integrator genes AtFT and AtSOC1 and increases expression of stress-responsive genes AtNHX1, AtSOS1 (salt) and AtCOR15A, AtRD20 and AtNHX1 (drought) in Arabidopsis.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Photoperiodic flowering regulatory pathway and abiotic stress (salt and drought) response pathway.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: MiCOL16B --(represses expression)--> AtFT; MiCOL16B --(represses expression)--> AtSOC1; MiCOL16B --(upregulates expression under salt stress)--> AtNHX1; MiCOL16B --(upregulates expression under salt stress)--> AtSOS1; MiCOL16B --(upregulates expression under drought stress)--> AtCOR15A; MiCOL16B --(upregulates expression under drought stress)--> AtRD20; MiCOL16B --(upregulates expression under drought stress)--> AtNHX1.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: CONSTANS-like (COL16) BBX family transcription factor with one B-box zinc finger domain and one CCT domain.
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus, as shown by 35S::MiCOL16B-GFP fusion transiently expressed in onion inner epidermal cells.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: In mango, MiCOL16B is expressed in all tested tissues, with highest levels in leaves and lowest in buds and flowers; expression in leaves of flowering branches is higher than in nonflowering branches and peaks during the early floral induction period before decreasing. MiCOL16B is expressed throughout the day but its transcript levels fluctuate without a clear diurnal rhythm under LD, MD and SD conditions.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Photoperiod conditions (LD 16 h light/8 h dark, MD 12 h light/12 h dark, SD 8 h light/16 h dark); salt stress using NaCl (100–200 mM in agar media, 300 mM in soil drench); osmotic/drought stress using mannitol (300–500 mM) and water withholding.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Flowering time regulation and abiotic stress tolerance in mango.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Flowering time; abiotic stress tolerance (salt stress, drought stress).
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Under LD, MiCOL16B overexpression lines flowered at about 26.9–28.4 days versus 24.8 days in WT and had 6.6–7.1 versus 5.5 rosette leaves; under SD, they flowered at 51.2–53.0 days versus 50.1 days in WT (Table 1). Under 300 mM NaCl, 73.3–93.3% of MiCOL16B overexpression plants survived versus 26.7% WT; under drought, 40.0–93.3% overexpression plants survived whereas WT plants did not survive.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Alters plant stature at flowering in Arabidopsis overexpression lines and increases primary root length under NaCl and mannitol treatments compared with WT.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: M. indica L. cv. SiJiMi tissues; wild-type Arabidopsis thaliana ecotype Columbia (Col-0); Arabidopsis 35S::MiCOL16B overexpression lines; onion inner epidermal cells; Y2H Gold yeast strain; Agrobacterium tumefaciens EH105.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Cloning of MiCOL16B from SiJiMi mango transcriptomic data and genomic DNA; sequence and domain analysis; qRT-PCR for tissue-specific, temporal and circadian expression; subcellular localization via 35S::MiCOL16B-GFP transient expression; yeast transcriptional activation assay with full-length and MR-deletion constructs; generation of Arabidopsis 35S::MiCOL16B lines by floral dip; flowering time, rosette leaf number and plant height measurements under LD and SD; root growth assays on MS with NaCl or mannitol; whole-plant salt (300 mM NaCl) and drought (water withholding) treatments with survival scoring; qRT-PCR of AtFT, AtSOC1, AtNHX1, AtRD20, AtSOS1 and AtCOR15A.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: 35S::MiCOL16B overexpression in Arabidopsis thaliana Col-0 under LD and SD conditions with flowering time and plant height measurements; salt and drought stress assays (NaCl, mannitol, water withholding) assessing root length and survival; qRT-PCR analysis of AtFT, AtSOC1 and stress-responsive genes; subcellular localization of MiCOL16B-GFP in onion epidermal cells; yeast transcriptional activation assay of BD-MiCOL16B and MR-deletion construct.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified as a CONSTANS-like homolog from SiJiMi mango transcriptomic data, then cloned from cDNA and genomic DNA of M. indica cv. SiJiMi using gene-specific primers and sequencing validation.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Useful target for adjusting flowering time and increasing salt and drought tolerance in mango breeding programs, since its overexpression delays flowering and confers enhanced abiotic stress tolerance in transgenic plants.
